_schema.purpose: wq-091 — disclosures feeding the Compute Ledger. Each component decomposes its AI revenue into three plain-English segments: (1) Frontier lab compute (Model Providers paying external hyperscalers/neoclouds for compute to train and serve their own foundation models — the circular-financing line), (2) AI workload compute (AI Natives, enterprises, model-serving infra cos, sovereign workloads buying raw cloud capacity), (3) Hosted model APIs (packaged token APIs — AWS Bedrock, Azure OpenAI Service, Google Vertex partner-served). Pass-through to model labs only exists in segment 3. Copilot-class revenue (M365 Copilot, GitHub Copilot, Copilot Studio, Gemini-in-Workspace) is scoped OUT and tracked separately. derive_compute_revenue.py reads this file and writes site-data.json:compute.* . Refresh quarterly within ~48h of each major hyperscaler earnings.
_schema.tiers: 1A = direct quotation from filed 10-K/10-Q. 1B = press release / earnings call narrative (own-mouth, including CEO disclosure such as Andy Jassy 2026 shareholder letter). 2A = editorial estimate with documented methodology (e.g., bottom-up segment sizing, AI portion of total Cloud). 2B = inferred from analyst consensus.
_schema.principal_agent_status: ALL FOUR (MSFT/AMZN/GOOGL/ORCL) confirmed PRINCIPAL (gross) treatment per data/methodology_constants.json:compute_revenue.principal_agent_treatment, verified 2026-05-06 from 10-K accounting policy notes. The 'principal-everywhere' assumption holds — gross sums are valid; Hosted model APIs net requires editorial pass-through estimation.
_schema.segment_model: Frontier lab compute: per-lab revenue × cloud-mix; the circular-financing line. AI workload compute: AI-native customers + enterprise model-serving cos + sovereign/regulated workloads buying raw cloud GPU/instance capacity. Hosted model APIs: hyperscaler resale of frontier models via Bedrock / Azure OpenAI Service / Vertex partner-served; pass-through % to labs applies here only. See methodology.html §Compute Revenue — three-segment model.
_schema.copilot_scope_out: Copilot-class revenue (M365 Copilot, GitHub Copilot, Copilot Studio for MSFT; Gemini-in-Workspace for GOOGL; equivalent embedded-AI productivity lines for AMZN/ORCL where identifiable) is per-seat productivity SaaS, not Compute. Scoped OUT of every aggregate published on /compute.html. Tracked-but-excluded; flows to a future Apps Ledger when that is built. Per-component figure surfaces in copilot_excluded_*_usd_b for transparency. Scope-out uses MS-declared values (not TAIL-derived) per dec-2026-05-06-compute-ledger-bucket-sizing.md §Issue 3.
_schema._caveat: All AI-attributable revenue lines for hyperscalers are editorial Tier 2A unless explicitly disclosed. MSFT discloses AI run-rate (Tier 1B). AMZN's Andy Jassy disclosed $15B Q1 2026 annualised run-rate (Tier 1B CEO disclosure) — anchor for AWS AI 2025. GOOGL discloses '13 product lines >$1B' / 'AI products grew 800%' narrative-only — quantum is bottom-up (Tier 2A). ORCL OCI AI portion is editorial. Refresh and tighten each cycle as disclosure granularity improves.

msft_ai.label: Microsoft Azure AI
msft_ai.segment_basis: Frontier lab compute ($18.0B 2025): OpenAI ~80% Azure-resident; OpenAI 2025 calendar compute spend ~$22B total (cash + prepaid commits, equity-funded burn); ~$18B Azure-resident is the central frontier-lab line on the page. Hosted model APIs ($1.5B 2025): Azure OpenAI Service to enterprise — bottom-up at ~50–100 mega-users at $2–10M each + ~200 production users at $500K–2M + pilot tail + PTU reservations + gov contracts (per dec-2026-05-06-compute-ledger-bucket-sizing.md §Issue 1). Pass-through 20% (Zitron-leaked rev-share methodology). AI workload compute ($1.1B): residual non-frontier enterprise + AI-Native + sovereign/regulated workloads on Azure. Copilot excluded $7.4B (MS-declared per D4): M365 Copilot list billings $5.4B (15M paid seats × $30/mo × 12) + GitHub Copilot $1.65B + Copilot Studio/Sales/Service ~$0.4B. Tie-out: 18.0+1.1+1.5+7.4 = $28.0B = MSFT-declared 2025 calendar AI line.

ai_run_rate_disclosure.source_quote: Microsoft's AI business surpassed an annual revenue run rate of $37 billion, up 123% year-over-year.
ai_run_rate_disclosure.source: Microsoft FY26 Q3 earnings press release / call commentary

msft_ai.notes: Segment-decomposed per wq-091 (deploy correction to wq-089). $28B 2025 calendar AI line holds (MSFT self-disclosed run-rate basis is the tightest of the four). Frontier lab compute dominates — OpenAI's compute spend on Azure is the central circular-financing line on the page. Copilot scope-out raised to MS-declared $7.4B per D4 (was $5.0B under wq-089). See dec-2026-05-06-compute-ledger-bucket-sizing.md §Issue 3 for full Copilot derivation.

googl_cloud_ai.label: Google Cloud AI
googl_cloud_ai.segment_basis: GCP 2025 AI line restated to $7B central (was $18B under wq-089) per bottom-up derivation in dec-2026-05-06-compute-ledger-bucket-sizing.md §Issue 5. Anchored on '13 product lines >$1B run-rate' commentary (Q2 2025): if 4–5 are predominantly AI-driven (Vertex AI Platform, Gemini API, AI Hypercomputer, AI agent/search products, AI-driven analytics) at $1.0–1.5B mid-2025 run-rate each = ~$5–7B; calendar 2025 averaging Q1–Q4 lands $6–8B central $7B. The $36.9B Q1 2026 Anthropic equity gain is investment income, NOT Cloud operating revenue (Fortune 2026-04-30) — does not inflate this line. Frontier lab compute ($3.5B 2025): Anthropic on TPUs $1.5–2B (deep partnership, April 2026 expanded multi-GW agreement from 2027); OpenAI on GCP $0.5–1B (CNBC 2025-07-16 partial-year diversification); Mistral + Cohere + AI21 + others ~$1B. Per D5, internal-recursion (Google's own Gemini compute on GCP) contributes $0 — the strict frontier-lab definition holds. Hosted model APIs ($0.5B): Vertex partner-served (smaller than Bedrock — Anthropic-on-Vertex newer, lower volume). Pass-through 20%. AI workload compute ($1.5B): non-frontier enterprise + AI-Native on GCP. Copilot excluded $1.5B (MS-declared analogue per D4): Gemini-in-Workspace external Apps revenue. The $1.5B reduction from prior $3B reclassifies to Frontier lab compute (Anthropic + OpenAI on GCP were under-sized) per Issue 6. Tie-out: 3.5+1.5+0.5+1.5 = $7.0B.

ai_run_rate_disclosure.narrative_quote: revenue from products built on Google's gen AI models grew nearly 800% year-over-year
ai_run_rate_disclosure.thirteen_product_lines_quote: 13 product lines now exceed $1B annual run rate
ai_run_rate_disclosure.source: Alphabet Q1 2026 earnings call commentary; AI portion of Cloud is bottom-up Tier 2A estimate (no discrete GCP AI revenue figure disclosed).

googl_cloud_ai.notes: Restated downward from $18B (wq-089) to $7B (wq-091) per D2 — bottom-up build vs prior 30% editorial AI-share weight on GCP. Frontier lab compute grew from $2B to $3.5B because Anthropic-on-TPUs and OpenAI-on-GCP were materially under-sized (Issue 6). Copilot dropped from $3B to $1.5B because the other $1.5B was actually Frontier lab compute mis-attributed as Workspace Apps. Per D5, internal-recursion (Google's own Gemini compute) does NOT contribute to Frontier lab compute — strict definition held. See dec-2026-05-06-compute-ledger-bucket-sizing.md §Issues 5 + 6.

amzn_aws_ai.label: AWS AI
amzn_aws_ai.segment_basis: AWS 2025 AI line restated to $10B (was $18B under wq-089) per D1, anchored on Andy Jassy's CEO disclosure of '$15B annualised AI run rate' Q1 2026 (Tier 1B). Working backward via AI/Anthropic growth pattern (steep ramp through 2025: Anthropic itself $1B Q1 → $5–6B Q4 run-rate): Q1 ~$1.5B, Q2 ~$2B, Q3 ~$2.75B, Q4 ~$3.5B → 2025 calendar central ~$10B; range $9–11B. Frontier lab compute ($7.0B 2025): Anthropic 2025 revenue $4.71B with compute spend exceeding revenue (subsidised by raised capital) — Anthropic on AWS infra ~60% (training + inference) ~$5–6B. Plus minor labs (Mistral via Bedrock partner, Cohere on AWS, AI21, smaller labs) ~$1–2B. Hosted model APIs ($2.5B 2025): Bedrock customer-paid resale of frontier models (Anthropic primarily, plus Llama/Mistral). Pass-through 25% blended (Anthropic-on-Bedrock terms differ from AOAI). AI workload compute ($0.5B): non-frontier enterprise + AI-Native residual; tighter than under wq-089 because Frontier lab compute is now sized correctly. Copilot excluded $0: AWS does not have a Copilot-equivalent named productivity line. Tie-out: 7.0+0.5+2.5+0 = $10.0B. CoreWeave double-count note: ~$3.8B of CoreWeave's $4.5B AI line is Microsoft-sub-rent for OpenAI compute — captured separately under msft_ai Frontier lab compute and under coreweave Frontier lab compute (same flow, different legs of ASC 606 principal treatment on each entity's P&L).

ai_run_rate_disclosure.narrative_quote: AWS's AI business is generating a $15 billion annualized revenue run rate.
ai_run_rate_disclosure.ceo_anchor_quote: Andy Jassy 2026 shareholder letter: AWS AI revenue crossed a $15B annualized run rate.
ai_run_rate_disclosure.source: Amazon CEO Andy Jassy 2026 shareholder letter (early 2026); plus Q1 2026 earnings call narrative — Bedrock client spending +170% Q-over-Q.

amzn_aws_ai.notes: Restated downward from $18B (wq-089) to $10B (wq-091) per D1 — Andy Jassy CEO disclosure of $15B Q1 2026 run-rate is the hard ceiling; 2025 calendar walks back to ~$10B via observed Anthropic growth shape. Tier annotations on Frontier lab compute / Hosted model APIs upgraded to 1B (CEO disclosure anchor). AWS-OpenAI Q1 2026 announcement of $38B + $100B/8yr expansion is RPO not realised revenue and does not affect 2025 segments. See dec-2026-05-06-compute-ledger-bucket-sizing.md §Issue 4.

oracle_cloud_ai.label: Oracle OCI AI
oracle_cloud_ai.segment_basis: Frontier lab compute ($2.0B 2025): OpenAI Stargate workload billing through OCI as data centers come online late 2025; partial-year contribution. The contracted $300B+ OpenAI commitment is RPO not realised revenue. Re-weighted upward from wq-089 ($1B) within the $3B AI line — Stargate ramp is principally external frontier-lab compute, not generic AI workload. Hosted model APIs ($0): Oracle does not run a token-API resale comparable to Bedrock/AOAI/Vertex. AI workload compute ($1.0B): general OCI AI workload — enterprise AI Native + research + model-serving cos. Copilot excluded $0: Oracle has no Copilot-class line. Tie-out: 2.0+1.0+0+0 = $3.0B. Update each cycle as Stargate billings ramp (Frontier lab compute will grow rapidly through 2026).

ai_run_rate_disclosure.narrative_quote: AI-attributable portion of OCI; Oracle does not separately disclose AI revenue. OCI growth narrative driven by 'unprecedented AI demand' and RPO disclosure of $300B+ OpenAI/Stargate commitment.
ai_run_rate_disclosure.source: Oracle Q3 FY26 earnings call commentary; AI-attributable line is editorial 2A estimate.

underlying_segment.note: Cloud revenue includes both OCI and SaaS (Fusion + NetSuite). OCI is the AI-relevant subset; AI portion of OCI is editorial.

oracle_cloud_ai.notes: ORCL fiscal year ends May; Q3 FY26 = Dec 2025 – Feb 2026 quarter. Tighten as Stargate revenue ramps through 2026. AI line $3B holds from wq-089; per-segment reallocation moves Stargate portion to Frontier lab compute (was AI workload compute under wq-089).

coreweave.label: CoreWeave
coreweave.segment_basis: Frontier lab compute ($4.0B 2025): ~85% of CoreWeave revenue is Microsoft sub-rent — much of which is OpenAI workload Microsoft sub-rents to CoreWeave to handle peak frontier-lab demand. The 'circular financing made visible' line on the Layer Stack: OpenAI pays Azure → Azure pays CoreWeave → both surface as compute revenue at the ecosystem level. Note — at OpenAI-line resolution this is the same flow as msft_ai Frontier lab compute; ASC 606 principal treatment on each entity's P&L means both are real revenue lines that aggregate gross-of-double-count by definition. AI workload compute ($0.5B): other AI Native + research customers on CoreWeave directly. No Hosted model APIs — pure-play GPU cloud, no token-API resale. Tie-out: 4.0+0.5+0+0 = $4.5B = S-1 / 10-Q disclosed.
coreweave.notes: CoreWeave IPO'd March 2025. The Microsoft-sub-rent-for-OpenAI line is editorially the most important callout for the Layer Stack — it's where the circular-financing geometry is most visible to a non-specialist reader. Per-segment reallocation under wq-091: Frontier lab compute up $0.2B / AI workload compute down $0.2B vs wq-089 — Microsoft-sub-rent share of total revenue is more concentrated on frontier-lab work than the prior 84%/16% split implied.

nebius.label: Nebius
nebius.segment_basis: Frontier lab compute ($0.65B 2025): Mistral and other smaller frontier labs renting capacity from Nebius (US-Europe footprint). AI workload compute ($0.30B): AI Native customers + research. No Hosted model APIs. Tie-out: 0.65+0.30+0+0 = $0.95B.
nebius.notes: Nebius (NBIS) — formerly Yandex N.V. assets, US-listed AI cloud. 6-K filer. Per-segment split is editorial 2A estimate; tighten if Nebius discloses customer concentration. Per-segment reallocation under wq-091: Frontier lab compute slightly higher / AI workload compute slightly lower vs wq-089 to align with neoclouds aggregate per Final Locked Table.

lambda.label: Lambda Labs
lambda.segment_basis: Frontier lab compute ($0.50B 2025): smaller frontier labs + research orgs renting GPU capacity from Lambda. AI workload compute ($0.10B): AI Native customers + sole-trader / academic. No Hosted model APIs. Tie-out: 0.50+0.10+0+0 = $0.60B.
lambda.notes: Private. Press-disclosed run rate; tighten if Lambda files S-1. Per-segment split is 2A editorial.

crusoe.label: Crusoe
crusoe.segment_basis: Frontier lab compute ($0.30B 2025): smaller labs + applied-research customers renting GPU capacity. AI workload compute ($0.10B): AI Native + general research. No Hosted model APIs. Tie-out: 0.30+0.10+0+0 = $0.40B.
crusoe.notes: Private. Press-disclosed. Per-segment split is 2A editorial.

yoy_reference._doc: 2024 sum-of-quarterlies for the YoY-growth headline (Box 4 on hero strip). Editorial Tier 2A — proportionally restated under wq-091 from $30B to $17B central as the editorial AI-share weights that overstated 2025 (per Issues 4 + 5 of dec-2026-05-06-compute-ledger-bucket-sizing.md) were equally overstated in 2024. Anchored against MSFT FY24 exit run-rate ~$13B (self-disclosed); AWS FY24 'multi-billion' narrative + Anthropic 2024 compute spend implies ~$3–4B; GCP FY24 bottom-up ~$2–3B (Anthropic on TPUs early stage, no OpenAI on GCP yet); ORCL FY24 < $1B (Stargate not yet billing); neoclouds ~$2B aggregate. Refresh when audited 10-Ks publish a tighter 2024 number.
yoy_reference.compute_revenue_2024_gross_source: Editorial 2A reconstruction from 2024 hyperscaler AI disclosures, restated under wq-091 to be consistent with corrected 2025 segment sizing. Methodology: MSFT FY24 exit run-rate ~$13B (self-disclosed); AWS FY24 ~$3–4B (Anthropic compute + minor labs, pre-Bedrock-scale); GCP FY24 ~$2–3B (no OpenAI on GCP yet, Anthropic-on-TPUs early); ORCL FY24 ~$0.5B (Stargate pre-billing); neoclouds ~$2B aggregate (CoreWeave pre-IPO public S-1 filings). Yields 2024 gross post-Copilot ~$15–18B central $17B. YoY 2025 vs 2024: ($45.55B − $17B) / $17B = +168%.

layer_stack_inputs._doc: Inputs for the Layer-Stack visual. All four layers on lookback 2025 actuals on the same time-basis (sum-of-quarterlies for compute, NVDA Data Center FY26 calendar 2025 for silicon, Power Ledger placeholder for power, live measured-cohort apps revenue from sankey for apps). Apps Revenue is published as the cohort total — explicitly not editorially extended to ecosystem scale. Bridge to ecosystem Apps is documented on /methodology.html as a future-Ledger gap, not a number on /compute.html. Compute Revenue net post-wq-091 lands at ~$44.5B (was $59.88B sum-of-quarterlies post-wq-089) — ~$20B downward restatement reflecting AMZN Jassy correction, GCP bottom-up, and Anthropic+OpenAI-on-GCP correctly sized.
layer_stack_inputs.apps_revenue_2025_source: Live read from site-data.json:sankey.totalCustomerRevenue (~$17.36B as of 2026-05-06) — the Revenue Ledger cohort total (Tier 1A-derived). NOT editorially multiplied to ecosystem scale; the cohort-vs-ecosystem gap is flagged on /methodology.html as a future-Apps-Ledger scope.
layer_stack_inputs.compute_revenue_2025_source: Sum of 2025 quarterly grosses minus 2025 Copilot-excluded total. Per Layer-Stack convention all four layers share a sum-of-quarterlies time basis. Under wq-091 segment sizing correction, 2025 compute net lands at ~$44.5B (was $59.88B under wq-089) — driven by AMZN AI line restated to $10B (Jassy CEO disclosure) and GOOGL AI line restated to $7B (bottom-up). See dec-2026-05-06-compute-ledger-bucket-sizing.md for full provenance.
layer_stack_inputs.silicon_revenue_2025_source: NVIDIA Data Center revenue FY26 (calendar 2025) ~$135B + AMD MI300/MI350 ~$5B + custom ASIC (Broadcom, Marvell) ~$25B
layer_stack_inputs.power_revenue_2025_source: Editorial — DC power + utilities AI-attributable. Power Ledger placeholder (wq-006).

_provenance.set_by: claude-code (wq-091 — segment sizing correction; AMZN $18B→$10B per Jassy; GCP $18B→$7B bottom-up; segment field renames bucket_*→plain English)
_provenance.supersedes: wq-089 (deploy correction; segment framework unchanged, per-provider sizing restated)
